transcript:
Right now you're looking at fourteen amber horizontal bands on near-black, each one lined with small glowing dots. It looks like a grid, almost like a spreadsheet or a sound spectrogram at rest — orderly, waiting.

But watch what happens when a band suddenly widens and throws off little curved traces. In the code, those are called "accommodation gestures" — arcs spawned by a function literally named "spawn accommodation arcs." Each band has a "hospitality coefficient," a number baked in at birth that determines how eager it is to expand and make room. Some channels are just constitutionally more welcoming than others — that's the artist's own phrase, in a comment right above where the coefficient is set. The widening isn't the channel filling with content; it's the channel making space for content that hasn't arrived yet. Even the fill rectangle underneath — the "prepared space" — has a comment saying emptiness must have visual weight.

So what looks like accumulating clutter is actually a record of readiness: every arc you see is a trace of a surface saying "here, this is for you," before it knows what "you" will be.
